body:

Hey Ibtesham,

Quick one for you today.

If you're using Claude Code, there's a command you might have missed: /loop

It's a lightweight scheduler that runs prompts at intervals, right inside your session.

No external tools.

No cron jobs.

Just type and go.

Examples:

/loop 5m check the deploy status

/loop 30s run tests

/loop 1h check for new GitHub issues

Why this matters:

You're deep in a refactor. You kicked off a deploy 10 minutes ago. Instead of context-switching to check the status, Claude just... tells you when it's done.

Or you're iterating on a feature. Tests running in background. /loop 30s run tests keeps you posted while you keep coding.

The pattern: Let Claude handle the monitoring. You focus on the work.

It stays active while your session is open.

Disable with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON if needed.

Bonus: They also shipped /effort this month—control how deep Claude thinks:

○ Low = quick answers

◐ Medium = balanced (default)

● High = deep analysis

Or just drop ultrathink in any prompt for maximum reasoning on that specific turn.

Small features. Big workflow impact.

Want to go deeper on AI development?

I'm building the AI Developer Masterclass—a comprehensive course covering LLM fundamentals, RAG systems, AI agents, and production patterns.

If you want to build AI applications that actually ship (not just demos), this is for you.

Join the waitlist for early access + launch pricing:

👉[Join the Masterclass Waitlist](https://www.vincimind.com/tr/2/3473440/13080599922/42184452/321652612ef3e0a5c3f318adb3bf6340d77a46861)

Talk soon,

Paulo

P.S. What's your most-used Claude Code command? Hit reply. I'm always curious what workflows people are building.

Vincibits 816 West Francis Ave #1110 Spokane, WA 99205, United States
[Unsubscribe](https://www.vincimind.com/s/unsubscribe-page/1/3473440/321652612ef3e0a5c3f318adb3bf6340d77a46861/13080599922)
Sent with [Systeme.io](https://systeme.io/?sa=sa002029930023b437c2d6259791f6685ef25f38bd)
ai_reply:
Subject: Re: Claude Code's /loop command just changed my workflow

Dear Paulo,

Thank you for reaching out to us about the /loop command in Claude Code. We appreciate your enthusiasm for the feature and its potential to improve your workflow. However, we couldn't find any specific information related to your query in our internal data. It seems that the email you sent is more of a newsletter or an update about the /loop command rather than a support request.

If you have any specific questions or issues related to Claude Code or any other product, please feel free to let us know and we'll be happy to assist you. We're here to help with any concerns or queries you may have.

Best regards,
Customer Support Team

subject: Update on PMP BLABS Improvements Project
body:
Hello Support Team,

I wanted to check the current status of the project "PMP BLABS (self)
improvements".

Could you please confirm the current progress and whether the project tasks
have been completed?

Also, please let me know if there are any pending updates required from our
side.

Thanks,
Ravi
ai_reply:
Subject: Update on PMP BLABS Improvements Project

Dear Ravi, hayyyyyyy brooo

Thank you for reaching out to us about the current status of the "PMP BLABS (self) improvements" project. We appreciate your interest in staying updated on the project's progress. After reviewing our internal records, we found a project titled "Changes and fixes needed" which was added on February 22, 2024, and completed on March 1, 2024. The project involved making changes and fixes as per the provided screenshots. However, we would like to clarify if this is the same project you are inquiring about.

If this is indeed the project you are referring to, please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns. If not, could you please provide us with more details or context about the "PMP BLABS (self) improvements" project so we can better assist you?

Best regards,
Customer Support Team

subject: Hackathon Launch: Measuring Progress Toward AGI: Cognitive Abilities
body:
Kaggle








Hi Ibteshaam,

Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) could transform science and help  
solve some of humanity's biggest challenges but measuring progress toward  
it remains difficult.

Today's AI models can achieve strong benchmark scores, often by recognizing  
patterns or recalling training data rather than demonstrating true  
reasoning. This makes it hard to tell when a model is actually solving a  
new problem.

In this hackathon, hosted with Google DeepMind, you'll design new  
benchmarks using Kaggle Benchmarks to evaluate core cognitive abilities  
such as learning, metacognition, attention, executive function and social  
cognition.





Total Prizes:

$200,000


Final Submission:

April 16, 2026




Learn More



Your work could help the AI community better understand the true  
capabilities and limitations of modern AI systems.

Good luck,

Martyna Plomecka
Kaggle Research Scientist



Kaggle, Inc 1600 Amphitheatre Pkwy Mountain View, CA 94043

This email was sent to ibtesham117@gmail.com because you indicated that  
you'd like to receive news and updates about Kaggle. If you don't want to  
receive these emails in the future, please unsubscribe here. You can also  
change your preferences on your account's profile page by logging in at  
kaggle.com.
ai_reply:
Subject: Hackathon Launch: Measuring Progress Toward AGI

Dear Customer,

We have received your email regarding the Hackathon Launch for measuring progress toward Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). The hackathon is hosted with Google DeepMind and aims to design new benchmarks to evaluate core cognitive abilities.

The project involves using Kaggle Benchmarks to assess abilities such as learning, metacognition, attention, executive function, and social cognition. The total prizes for the hackathon are $200,000, and the final submission is due on April 16, 2026.

However, we could not find any specific information related to your query in our records. Could you please provide more details or clarify your question so we can assist you better?

Best regards,
Customer Support Team

subject: Competition Launch: BirdCLEF+ 2026
body:
Kaggle








Hi Ibteshaam,

What can a wetland tell us if we listen closely enough?

The Pantanal—the world's largest tropical wetland spanning 150,000 km²—is  
under threat from wildfires and climate change. With over 650 species and  
vast remote stretches, traditional field surveys simply cannot scale to  
meet the urgency of conservation.

In this competition, you'll develop models to identify understudied species  
from their vocalizations in Pantanal audio recordings, turning sound into  
tools to support biodiversity monitoring.





Total Prizes:

$50,000


Entry Deadline:

May 27, 2026




Learn More



Your work will help bring these voices into today's conservation efforts  
and support new methods for identifying species that have long been  
overlooked.


Good luck,

Ryan Holbrook
Kaggle Data Scientist



Kaggle, Inc 1600 Amphitheatre Pkwy Mountain View, CA 94043

This email was sent to ibtesham117@gmail.com because you indicated that  
you'd like to receive news and updates about Kaggle. If you don't want to  
receive these emails in the future, please unsubscribe here. You can also  
change your preferences on your account's profile page by logging in at  
kaggle.com.

subject: Quick question for you
body:
Two taps to help make Suno even better     Hi there,

We'd love to learn a bit more about you - just two quick
questions that'll take less than a minute.

Your input helps us build tools that feel right for the way you
create.

Thanks for being here.

subject: ✏️ I tried describing a song to Suno. Now I’m emotionally attached.
body:
Turns out, all I had to do was say it out loud (into a box). You gotta try this. ( https://clicks.suno.com/f/a/We6jp9YZstgKtVlI-62Hlw~~/AAQRxRA~/8zoXdwZrHqJsFnk6Dhf886EyYSoBH121QJMmHxuzWnMZRqEfOBVf6IMRUJDP3pABZgvb7qSX3hL3ZlojgGlUPfAPkWYk62H55x3aosBh3BQyUM-gmMPmGkEMJdzggyJK ) Okay, confession time. I once spent six months learning guitar just to write one (1) song for my college crush. It was… fine. She didn’t call me back.

Now? I can just say what I want and whip up a full song with Suno—no missed chords, no heartbreak, no tragic open mic nights.

And here’s the trick: the more detail you give, the better it gets. Add context. Mention the music style. Even toss in a lyric or two.

Like this:
🎵 “A 90s R&B slow jam about realizing your cat is your best friend.”
🎵 “An indie folk anthem about switching careers and moving to a lighthouse.”
🎵 “A stadium rock song about running into your ex at the grocery store, with the line ‘aisle five flashbacks.’”

That’s the magic of Suno’s one-box prompt — you bring the idea, and we help you turn it into something unforgettable. 🎶 Describe Your Song, Hear It Now 🎶 ( https://clicks.suno.com/f/a/HBOQXpX8JhUpcmsPeWoLBw~~/AAQRxRA~/8zoXdwZrHqJsFnk6Dhf886EyYSoBH121QJMmHxuzWnPZBZlLijWeJkxUmD7CGd8aK19dR-f2gVSME2sYpEe-aoNN3gDDXnXtvwBFGc3lbl0LMM8-W2dR0Fa7yThxyKbm ) ( https://clicks.suno.com/f/a/yx463utBbwYH-FkzJ_lMiw~~/AAQRxRA~/8zoXdwZrHqJsFnk6Dhf886EyYSoBH121QJMmHxuzWnMtE-Ha_M__0Z54iGMt7wkASv5n-5FIdO44FTVzszn9p43PjbfghejL1oyTs-rq0SQGUY1U6uDpd_EVZ8KX3oL- ) ( https://clicks.suno.com/f/a/NerbhLoFOQMYi_bMLtkPDg~~/AAQRxRA~/8zoXdwZrHqJsFnk6Dhf886EyYSoBH121QJMmHxuzWnOTUVGQ8z2GaLoJCzQ7bpIXkvjhAoWlA1t6YQKQjeHJR0JG0RdLp0-FSVLhcqTMgXbBn-96HFjivMd1rXO8V_dW ) TikTok ( https://clicks.suno.com/f/a/kVyn0vQOr2jHttY18iX1uA~~/AAQRxRA~/2aJhJZ6C8IRaH2-gI7eO97lNnyvgNLuCBAB4DJQEuhHCoCz3pgAwGX2Pe0i3U5q-2ETTf2rqaliER2YdzYyiSKXSA8q0RLPzatMZORQPVVb_vjuc5747CV2qmKSGd-kP87Zr-OOAQnRkkYnxOglkqA~~ ) X ( https://clicks.suno.com/f/a/B5msi4JRTTlN72pmmzvKtA~~/AAQRxRA~/8zoXdwZrHqJsFnk6Dhf882ZebS3R3WYW01zzPZbd_UinXWnLXYVXbrRWBa6eWNawqXmxMUnCnAkin_wZ6f_QaLsdemGitKqCen0sLscEhpCx0fQHN3IB_79qTWp4gJhD ) Instagram ( https://clicks.suno.com/f/a/isH1x-6jrOWU8tICSR5N9g~~/AAQRxRA~/yx_okUvc9UEw8k5zlfueZMoXd9gaGp5tqS6KJsPGsSmUGn5mh8kzHAvUcwwM1-eMnK57tljuCBpDf8lPrE3x46Kp_Eo50S-3_yU2IgVFI_n84QSYU4NRsbA9da0ZcvZhcZ0Dggy6MV4296qVkDNItw~~ ) No longer want to receive these emails? subject: Welcome to Suno — Transform your ideas into original music
body:
Explore music creation through natural language ( ) Thank you for joining Suno.

Traditional music creation often requires instruments, collaborators, or technical knowledge. Suno removes those barriers.

By simply describing a theme or idea, Suno generates a complete song — allowing you to create without limits.

For example:
🎵 “A classical piece inspired by the changing seasons”
🎵 “An ambient track evoking peaceful solitude”
🎵 “A soulful composition exploring personal growth”

We’re excited to help you bring your musical vision to life.
